# Beehiiv vs Substack vs Ghost - 2026 Comparison

## Summary

Structured three-way comparison covering pricing, feature strengths, and use-case fit. Ranks Beehiiv first for most creators in 2026, Ghost second for independence and blogging, Substack third for beginners and pure writers.

## Key quotations / statistics

- "Beehiiv paid plans start at around $43/month (Scale) for the full suite — including a built-in ad network, boosts for cross-promotions, email automations, A/B testing, surveys, and advanced segmentation."
- "0% platform fee on paid subscriptions (you only pay standard Stripe processing fees of about 2.9% + $0.30)."
- "Substack takes a 10% cut on paid subscriptions, plus Stripe fees."
- "Ghost Pro (managed hosting) starts at $15/month for the Starter plan (1,000 members)" up to $199/month for Business.
- "Real-world tests in 2026 (from creator reports and benchmarks) often show Beehiiv pulling ahead in revenue growth for mid-sized lists thanks to its ad and referral ecosystem."
- "Ghost users report higher lifetime value per subscriber because of better branding and SEO traffic."

## Platform strengths matrix

| Platform | Best For | Key Advantage | Key Weakness |
|---|---|---|---|
| Beehiiv | Newsletter operators growing a business | Ad network + referrals + 0% fees | Less community/discovery than Substack |
| Substack | Beginners, pure writers | Discovery engine, network effects, zero friction | 10% revenue cut, limited customization |
| Ghost | Technical creators, SEO-focused | Full customization, 0% fees, SEO, self-host option | No growth tools, requires tech skills |

## Pricing summary (Jan 2026)

- Substack: Free until paid subscribers, then 10% commission + Stripe fees
- Ghost Pro: $18/month Starter (500 subs), scaling by subscriber count; 0% transaction fees
- Beehiiv: Free to 2,500, then ~$43/month Scale (up to 100,000 subs)

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- The three-platform matrix belongs in `guides/00-platform-selection.md` as the core decision frame.
- Substack's position: best as a starting point, not optimal for scaling. The "start here, graduate to Beehiiv or Ghost" pattern is documented here.
- Ghost's SEO advantage (higher LTV per subscriber) is relevant for the "Ghost for content-first operators" recommendation.
- The 10% Substack cut compound effect at scale ($100K/year in subscriptions = $10K going to Substack) is the key migration trigger.
